content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all four pending amendments for planned-elsewhere reconciliation correctness. Amendment 1: Changed record_planned_elsewhere_mapping to downgrade NDJSON sidecar write failures to a warning log instead of propagating via `?`, consistent with the docstring's 'secondary' semantics and the fact that load_planned_elsewhere_mappings now reads from the authoritative journal, not the sidecar. Amendment 2: Updated build_voter_prompt and build_arbiter_prompt in final_review.rs to include a 'Classification: planned-elsewhere — mapped to bead X' note in each amendment's text when mapped_to_bead_id is Some, so reviewers (planner, voters, arbiter) see and can evaluate the planned-elsewhere claim before their vote decides whether the active bead restarts. Amendment 3: Gated verified-mapping persistence on sync_flush success — the loop in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success now only writes a verified=true record when either no comment was posted for that outcome, or the comment was posted AND the flush succeeded. If flush fails but a comment was posted, the mapping stays unverified so replay will re-verify and re-attempt the comment. Amendment 4: Narrowed the BrExitError match in verify_bead_exists to only treat exits whose stderr/stdout contain 'bead not found' or 'issue not found' as definitive staleness, matching the existing br_show_error_is_missing pattern in cli/run.rs. Other non-zero exits (corrupt state, permission errors) are now treated as TransientError for operator investigation. Updated mock in comment_skipped_for_stale_bead test to use realistic 'bead not found' message. Added non_missing_bead_exit_error_is_transient_not_stale test.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: In record_planned_elsewhere_mapping (service.rs), changed the NDJSON append_mapping call from propagating errors via `?` to logging a warning and continuing. Updated docstring comment to match.
2. [Completed] Amendment 2: In build_voter_prompt (final_review.rs), added a planned-elsewhere classification note to each amendment's text when mapped_to_bead_id is Some, explaining that accepting it will not trigger a restart and the concern is recorded as covered by the mapped-to bead.
3. [Completed] Amendment 2: In build_arbiter_prompt (final_review.rs), added the same planned-elsewhere classification note for disputed amendments with mapped_to_bead_id.
4. [Completed] Amendment 3: In verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success (success_reconciliation.rs), changed sync_flush to return a flush_succeeded boolean. The verified-mapping persistence loop now only writes verified=true when (!outcome.comment_posted || flush_succeeded), preventing permanent loss of unposted comments.
5. [Completed] Amendment 4: In verify_bead_exists (planned_elsewhere.rs), narrowed the BrExitError match to inspect stderr/stdout for 'bead not found' or 'issue not found' patterns. Only matching messages produce Stale; other non-zero exits produce TransientError. Updated comment_skipped_for_stale_bead mock to use realistic 'bead not found: ghost-bead' message.
6. [Completed] Added non_missing_bead_exit_error_is_transient_not_stale test — verifies that a non-zero exit with a non-missing-bead message ('internal error: corrupt bead index') propagates as a transient error instead of being silently treated as stale.
7. [Completed] Run cargo test (758 passed: 683 lib + 174 integration + 1 unit, 0 failed), cargo fmt --check (clean), cargo clippy (no new warnings).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 758 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (683 lib, 174 integration, 1 unit, 9 doc-tests ignored)
- cargo test --lib non_missing_bead_exit: 1 new test passes (non_missing_bead_exit_error_is_transient_not_stale)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file; all 19 warnings are pre-existing
- Amendment 1 verified: record_planned_elsewhere_mapping now logs NDJSON failure as warning and returns Ok; callers no longer see an error when the secondary write fails
- Amendment 2 verified: build_voter_prompt and build_arbiter_prompt both append 'Classification: planned-elsewhere — mapped to bead X' note when mapped_to_bead_id is Some; planner (which reuses build_voter_prompt) also sees the note
- Amendment 3 verified: flush_succeeded boolean gates the verified-mapping persistence loop; if any_comments is true and sync_flush fails, outcomes with comment_posted=true are NOT written as verified=true, ensuring replay will re-attempt
- Amendment 4 verified: BrExitError in verify_bead_exists now inspects stderr/stdout for missing-bead patterns; non-matching exits produce TransientError; existing tests updated and new test confirms behavior
- Files modified: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs (NDJSON error downgrade), src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs (PE notes in prompts), src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs (flush gate), src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs (narrowed stale detection + new test)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change
- One flaky integration test (checkpoint_test::worktree_adapter_creates_finds_and_resets_checkpoint_commits) occasionally fails due to git timing; unrelated to this change
- The planned-elsewhere classification note in final-review prompts is appended to the amendment body as markdown text; reviewers could theoretically vote to accept the concern while rejecting the planned-elsewhere classification, but the system treats any acceptance of a mapped_to_bead_id amendment as planned-elsewhere. This is acceptable because the mapping field is set by the original review stage, not by the final-review voter
- The br_show_error_is_missing pattern is duplicated between cli/run.rs and planned_elsewhere.rs; a future consolidation could extract this into a shared utility on BrError
